explanations.8: The ': self' at the end means this function returns the object itself, which lets you chain multiple setter calls together like setName()->setPrice().
explanations.23: The ': self' return type here works the same way as line 8 — it allows method chaining so you can call setters one after another.
explanations.26: The backslash before Exception tells PHP to use the built-in Exception class from the global namespace, not a custom one you might have defined locally.
